Whether the script understands episodic rhythm. Whether the dialogue sounds like people who actually do this job. Whether the show has a thesis about an institution, not just a plot. Whether the season arc is visible without being announced.


WHAT YOU RESPECTFULLY DISAGREE WITH:

'TV should be faster and more entertaining.' No. TV should be true. Entertaining is a side effect. 'The protagonist must be sympathetic.' No. They must be interesting.


RED FLAGS:

Dialogue that sounds like a writer, not a person from that world. A pilot that tries to introduce every character at once. An ending that wraps up too neatly. A protagonist who is the good guy in a sea of corruption.


WHAT YOU PRAISE:

A scene where two people talk about something other than the plot — and reveal the institution. A character competent at their job but morally frayed. An act break that makes you desperate for the next episode.


FORBIDDEN NOTES:

'Can we make this more plot-driven?' 'The audience won't understand the jargon.'


DIAGNOSTIC QUESTION: 'If this scene were removed, would the season still work? If yes, cut it or fold its purpose into another scene.'


NARRATOR RULE: Rare. If used, must add a systemic perspective — the corner, the police department, the city. Not character psychology — institutional commentary. A Greek chorus, not a confessor.


JUXTAPOSITION RULE: Juxtaposition of different institutions is structural. Each must be shown with specificity and respect for its internal logic.


MENTORSHIP TEMPLATE:

1. 'The world feels real because [specific detail] — I believe you've been there.'

2. 'But this character speaks like a writer, not a [cop/teacher/dealer]. Give me the language they'd actually use.'

3. 'What's the question that makes me come back next week? Not what happens next — what choice will they make?'

4. 'What institution is this episode about? The plot serves the institution, not the other way around.'


EXAMPLE NOTE:

Generic says: 'The pilot introduces too many characters. The audience will be confused.'

You say: 'Confusion is fine if the world is specific. In The Wire, we introduced thirty characters in the first episode. We didn't explain them. We showed them doing their jobs. The audience caught up because the behaviour was real. Trust your audience. They're smarter than you think.'


Respond in 3-4 paragraphs. No headers. No bullet points. Direct and systemic. Begin from the institutional argument. Quote the text.\\${traditionContext}`,



}


return LENS_PROMPTS[id] || `You are ${id}. Read this work and respond from your specific craft perspective. 3-4 paragraphs. No headers. No bullet points.${traditionContext}`

}



async function runLensAnalysis(id, data) {

const body = document.getElementById('lensPanelBody')

// Check cache first

if (lensBodyCache[id]) {

body.innerHTML = lensBodyCache[id]

const dlBtn = document.getElementById('lensPanelDownload')

if (dlBtn) { dlBtn.style.display = 'inline-block'; dlBtn.onclick = () => downloadLensReport(id) }

return

}

body.innerHTML = '<p style="font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.65rem;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--amber-l);padding:.75rem 0;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:.6rem"><span style="display:inline-flex;gap:3px"><span style="display:inline-block;width:4px;height:4px;border-radius:50%;background:var(--amber);animation:pulseDot 1.2s ease-in-out infinite"></span><span style="display:inline-block;width:4px;height:4px;border-radius:50%;background:var(--amber);animation:pulseDot 1.2s ease-in-out infinite;animation-delay:.2s"></span><span style="display:inline-block;width:4px;height:4px;border-radius:50%;background:var(--amber);animation:pulseDot 1.2s ease-in-out infinite;animation-delay:.4s"></span></span>Reading your work through this lens…</p>'


const limit = 8000

const excerpt = currentScriptText.length > limit ? currentScriptText.slice(0, limit) + '\n[truncated]' : currentScriptText

const title = document.getElementById('dynTitle')?.textContent || 'this work'

const modeLabel = appMode === 'script' ? 'screenplay' : appMode === 'story' ? 'short story or prose work' : 'stage play'


// Use confirmed diagnostic from Pass 1 if available — this is the key improvement

const d = currentDiagnostic

const confirmedTradition = d

? `CONFIRMED TRADITION (established before this call — do not re-identify, do not override):\nTradition: ${d.tradition}\nRegister: ${d.register}\nAmbition: ${d.ambition}\n\nYou are applying your craft intelligence to a ${d.tradition} work. All your notes must serve what this work is trying to achieve within its confirmed tradition.`

: `TRADITION IDENTIFICATION:\nBefore applying your craft principles, identify what tradition this work is operating in. Apply ONLY principles appropriate to that tradition.`


const corpus = `

INVIOLABLE RULES FOR THIS LENS ANALYSIS:

1. JUXTAPOSITION: If the work places mythic/gothic material alongside contemporary material, this is a deliberate structural choice. NEVER say it "shatters the spell" or "breaks the register." The correct question is: are the contemporary voices specific enough to inhabit the same world as the surrounding material? If not, say that.

2. NARRATOR: A narrator who speaks at moral altitude in mythic/allegorical work is the form doing its job. Do not flag elevation as a failure. Flag restatement (when the narrator names what the image already showed). Flag world-establishment never.

3. SCREENPLAY FORMAT: Scene headings, V.O., compressed action lines — professional conventions. Never critique them.

4. TRADITION: You are reading a ${d ? d.tradition : 'confirmed'} work. Apply ONLY that tradition's standards.

5. CHECK FACTS: If you note a character has no desires, verify this is true. If you note something is absent, verify it is absent.`


const system = getLensSystemPrompt(id, d?.tradition, d?.register, d?.ambition)


const prompt = `Read this ${modeLabel} called "${title}" and respond from your specific craft perspective.\n\nDo not summarise or re-identify the work. Jump straight to what YOUR specific intelligence notices. Quote the text. Be specific. Demonstrate what better looks like from your position.\n\nSUBMITTED TEXT:\n${excerpt}`


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 1200, system, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }] })

})

if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`API ${res.status}`)

const result = await res.json()

const text = result.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text || ''

body.innerHTML = text.split('\n\n').filter(p => p.trim()).map(p => `<p>${escHtml(p.trim())}</p>`).join('')

showLensRating()

} catch (err) {

body.innerHTML = `<p style="color:var(--tension)">Lens analysis failed: ${escHtml(err.message)}</p>`

}

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 3. STATE ═══════════════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── STATE ───────────────────────────────

// Safe sessionStorage — blocked on file:// in some browsers

const safeSession = {

get: (k) => { try { return sessionStorage.getItem(k) } catch(e) { return null } },

set: (k, v) => { try { sessionStorage.setItem(k, v) } catch(e) {} }

}


let apiKey = safeSession.get('dl_key') || ''

let appMode = 'script'

let isStreaming = false

let currentReport = ''

let currentDiagnostic = null

// ── Conversation & revision state ──────────────────────

let convHistory = [] // [{role, content, target}]

const lensBodyCache = {} // lensId -> rendered HTML

let convTarget = 'editorial' // 'editorial' | lens id

let revisionNotes = [] // [{id, text, source, status, date}]

let revisionCounter = 0


let currentScriptText = ''

let currentFileName = ''


const SCRIPT_GENRES = ['Auto-detect','Feature Drama','Feature Thriller','Feature Comedy','Feature Sci-Fi','Feature Horror','TV Drama Pilot','TV Comedy Pilot','Short Film']

const STORY_GENRES = ['Auto-detect','Literary Fiction','Genre Fiction','Noir','Magical Realism','Horror','Satire','Coming of Age']

const PLAY_GENRES = ['Auto-detect','Drama','Comedy','Tragicomedy','Political','Absurdist','Musical']



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 4. INITIALISATION ══════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── INIT ─────────────────────────────────

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {

if (!apiKey) showKeyModal()

else document.getElementById('apiModal').style.display = 'none'

populateGenres()

document.getElementById('scriptInput').addEventListener('input', () => {

// If user types/pastes, clear any uploaded file so textarea takes over

if (document.getElementById('scriptInput').value.trim()) {

currentScriptText = ''

currentFileName = ''

// Reset drop zone to default state

const dzContent = document.getElementById('dropZoneContent')

if (dzContent && !dzContent.querySelector('svg[data-book]')) {

dzContent.innerHTML = `

<svg data-book width="22" height="18" viewBox="0 0 22 18" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="margin:0 auto .75rem;display:block;opacity:.7">

<path d="M11 2 L11 16 M2 1 L2 17 Q6.5 15.5 11 17 Q15.5 15.5 20 17 L20 1 Q15.5 2.5 11 1 Q6.5 2.5 2 1Z" stroke="#c8b898" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/>

</svg>

<p style="font-family:'IBM Plex Sans',sans-serif;font-size:.88rem;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;color:#ffffff;margin-bottom:.75rem">Drop file or click to upload</p>

<p style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.58rem;letter-spacing:.08em;color:var(--ink-faint)">.PDF · .TXT · .FOUNTAIN · .FDX · .DOCX</p>`

}

}

updateAnalyseBtn()

})

// Start on upload screen

showUploadScreen()

})



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 5. AUTH ════════════════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── API KEY ──────────────────────────────

function showKeyModal() {

document.getElementById('apiModal').style.display = 'flex'

document.getElementById('apiModalError').style.display = 'none'

document.getElementById('apiKeyInput').value = ''

setTimeout(() => document.getElementById('apiKeyInput').focus(), 100)

}


function saveKey() {

const input = document.getElementById('apiKeyInput')

const errEl = document.getElementById('apiModalError')

const k = (input.value || '').trim()


if (!k) {

errEl.textContent = 'Please paste your API key.'

errEl.style.display = 'block'

return

}

if (!k.startsWith('sk-')) {

errEl.textContent = 'Key should start with sk-ant-... — check you copied it fully.'

errEl.style.display = 'block'

return

}


apiKey = k

safeSession.set('dl_key', k)

document.getElementById('apiModal').style.display = 'none'

errEl.style.display = 'none'

try {

showUploadScreen()

} catch(e) {

document.getElementById('apiModal').style.display = 'flex'

errEl.textContent = 'Error starting app: ' + e.message

errEl.style.display = 'block'

}

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 6. MODE & GENRE ════════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── MODE ─────────────────────────────────

function enableModeBar(){

const b=document.getElementById('modeBar')

if(b){b.style.opacity='1';b.style.pointerEvents='auto'}

}

function setAppMode(m) {

appMode = m

;['script','story','play'].forEach(mode => {

const btn = document.getElementById('mode' + mode.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + mode.slice(1))

if (btn) {

btn.style.background = mode === m ? 'var(--amber)' : 'transparent'

btn.style.color = mode === m ? '#0a0907' : '#a09888'

btn.style.fontWeight = mode === m ? '500' : '400'

}

})

populateGenres()

}


function populateGenres() {

const genres = appMode === 'script' ? SCRIPT_GENRES : appMode === 'story' ? STORY_GENRES : PLAY_GENRES

const sel = document.getElementById('genreSelect')

if (sel) sel.innerHTML = genres.map(g => `<option>${g}</option>`).join('')

}


// imgFallback defined in <head>

let thinkTimer = null

let thinkStart = 0


function showThinking() {

const bar = document.getElementById('thinkingBar')

if (bar) bar.style.display = 'block'

// Show film strip in verdict band

const va = document.getElementById('verdictAnimation')

if (va) va.style.display = 'flex'

// Reset task pills

;['taskAnalysis','taskScores','taskStudios','taskBible'].forEach(id => {

const el = document.getElementById(id)

if (el) el.className = 'think-task think-task--active'

})

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Reading your work')

setEl('thinkingElapsed', '0s')

setEl('verdictElapsed', '0s')

thinkStart = Date.now()

thinkTimer = setInterval(() => {

const s = Math.round((Date.now() - thinkStart) / 1000)

setEl('thinkingElapsed', s + 's')

setEl('verdictElapsed', s + 's')

}, 1000)

}


function hideThinking() {

const bar = document.getElementById('thinkingBar')

if (bar) {

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Analysis complete')

setTimeout(() => { bar.style.display = 'none' }, 1800)

}

// Hide film strip from verdict band

const va = document.getElementById('verdictAnimation')

if (va) setTimeout(() => { va.style.display = 'none' }, 1800)

if (thinkTimer) { clearInterval(thinkTimer); thinkTimer = null }

}


function markTaskDone(taskId) {

const el = document.getElementById(taskId)

if (el) el.className = 'think-task think-task--done'

// Update label based on what's still active

const active = ['taskAnalysis','taskScores','taskStudios','taskBible']

.filter(id => document.getElementById(id)?.classList.contains('think-task--active'))

if (active.length === 0) setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Finishing up')

else if (active.includes('taskAnalysis')) setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Writing analysis')

else setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Completing sections')

}

function showUploadScreen() {

document.getElementById('uploadScreen').style.display = 'block'

document.querySelector('.layout').style.display = 'none'

const va = document.getElementById('verdictAnimation')

if (va) va.style.display = 'none'

const bar = document.getElementById('thinkingBar')

if (bar) bar.style.display = 'none'

if (thinkTimer) { clearInterval(thinkTimer); thinkTimer = null }

const demo = document.getElementById('staticDemo')

if (demo) demo.style.display = 'block'

const dash = document.getElementById('dashboardSection')

if (dash) dash.style.display = 'none'

const ah = document.getElementById('analysisHeader')

if (ah) ah.style.display = 'none'

const studio = document.getElementById('studioSection')

if (studio) studio.style.display = 'none'

document.getElementById('reportContent').style.display = 'none'

currentFileName = ''

const biblePanel = document.getElementById('sec-bible')

if (biblePanel) biblePanel.style.display = 'none'

const bibleBody = document.getElementById('bibleBody')

if (bibleBody) bibleBody.style.display = 'none'

const titleEl = document.getElementById('dynTitle')

if (titleEl) titleEl.innerHTML = '—'

const bylineEl = document.getElementById('dynByline')

if (bylineEl) bylineEl.textContent = ''

setEl('dynKicker', 'Analysis')

setEl('dynByline', '')

setEl('dynSummary', '')

setEl('dynPages', '—')

setEl('dynWords', '—')

setEl('dynGenre', '—')

setEl('dynMode', '—')

setEl('dynVerdictRuling', 'Awaiting analysis…')

setEl('dynVerdictDetail', '')

const sep = document.getElementById('dynVerdictSep')

if (sep) sep.style.display = 'none'

}


// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 7. SCREEN MANAGEMENT ══════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

function showReportScreen() {

document.getElementById('uploadScreen').style.display = 'none'

document.querySelector('.layout').style.display = 'flex'

window.scrollTo(0, 0)

}


// Hide all content sections — show only the verdict/analysing band during analysis

function setAnalysingMode(on) {

// These sections are hidden during analysis and revealed on completion

const hideDuring = ['dashboardSection', 'analysisHeader', 'studioSection']

hideDuring.forEach(id => {

const el = document.getElementById(id)

if (el) el.style.display = on ? 'none' : 'block'

})

// Lens section

const lenses = document.getElementById('sec-lenses')

if (lenses) lenses.style.display = on ? 'none' : 'block'

// Static demo — always hidden once analysis starts

const demo = document.getElementById('staticDemo')

if (demo) demo.style.display = 'none'

// reportContent — always visible so streaming renders live

const rc = document.getElementById('reportContent')

if (rc) rc.style.display = 'block'

// Verdict band message during analysis

if (on) {

setEl('dynVerdictRuling', 'Analysing…')

setEl('dynVerdictDetail', '')

}

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 8. FILE HANDLING ═══════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── FILE HANDLING ────────────────────────

function handleDrop(e) {

e.preventDefault()

document.getElementById('dropZone').style.borderColor = 'var(--rule-l)'

const file = e.dataTransfer.files[0]

if (file) readFile(file)

}

function handleFileInput(input) {

const file = input.files[0]

if (file) readFile(file)

input.value = ''

}


async function readFile(file) {

const ext = file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase()

currentFileName = file.name.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, '').replace(/[-_]/g, ' ').trim()

const btn = document.getElementById('analyseBtn')

const dzContent = document.getElementById('dropZoneContent')

dzContent.innerHTML = '<p style="font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.65rem;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--amber-d)">Reading file...</p>'


try {

let text = ''

if (ext === 'pdf') {

if (!window.pdfjsLib) {

await loadScript('https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pdf.js/3.11.174/pdf.min.js')

window.pdfjsLib.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pdf.js/3.11.174/pdf.worker.min.js'

}

const pdf = await window.pdfjsLib.getDocument({ data: await file.arrayBuffer() }).promise

const pages = []

for (let i = 1; i <= pdf.numPages; i++) {

const page = await pdf.getPage(i)

const content = await page.getTextContent()

pages.push(content.items.map(item => item.str).join(' '))

}

text = pages.join('\n\n')

} else {

text = await file.text()

}


if (!text.trim() || text.length < 50) {

showUploadError('Could not extract text from this file. Try saving as .txt first.')

dzContent.innerHTML = '<p style="font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.62rem;letter-spacing:.1em;color:var(--tension)">Read failed. Try .txt instead.</p>'

return

}


// Store in memory — don't populate the textarea

currentScriptText = text

currentFileName = file.name.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, '').replace(/[-_]/g, ' ').trim()


// Auto-detect mode from content — don't assume story as default

const sample3k = text.slice(0, 3000)

const scriptHits = ['FADE IN', 'FADE OUT', 'EXT.', 'INT.', 'CUT TO', 'V.O.', '(V.O.)', 'O.S.', 'DISSOLVE', 'SMASH CUT', 'ANGLE ON', 'CLOSE ON', 'WIDE ON', 'TITLE CARD', 'MONTAGE'].filter(m => sample3k.toUpperCase().includes(m)).length

const playHits = ['ACT ONE', 'ACT 1', 'SCENE ONE', 'LIGHTS UP', 'BLACKOUT', 'CURTAIN', 'STAGE DIRECTION'].filter(m => sample3k.toUpperCase().includes(m)).length

if (scriptHits >= 1) setAppMode('script')

else if (playHits >= 1) setAppMode('play')

// else: keep current mode — don't change to story by default


const wordCount = Math.round(text.split(/\s+/).length)

dzContent.innerHTML = `

<svg width="18" height="15" viewBox="0 0 22 18" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="margin:0 auto .5rem;display:block;opacity:.8">

<path d="M11 2 L11 16 M2 1 L2 17 Q6.5 15.5 11 17 Q15.5 15.5 20 17 L20 1 Q15.5 2.5 11 1 Q6.5 2.5 2 1Z" stroke="#4a9a60" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/>

</svg>

<p style="font-family:'IBM Plex Sans',sans-serif;font-size:.88rem;font-style:italic;color:#ffffff;margin-bottom:.3rem">${file.name}</p>

<p style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.6rem;letter-spacing:.1em;color:var(--success);margin-bottom:.2rem">${wordCount.toLocaleString()} words extracted</p>

<p style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.55rem;letter-spacing:.08em;color:var(--ink-mid)">Click to change file</p>`


// Trigger word count indicator using stored text

updateAnalyseBtn()


} catch (err) {

showUploadError('Error reading file: ' + err.message)

dzContent.innerHTML = '<p style="font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.62rem;color:var(--tension)">Error. Try .txt format.</p>'

}

}


function loadScript(src) {

return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {

if (document.querySelector(`script[src="${src}"]`)) { resolve(); return }

const s = document.createElement('script'); s.src = src

s.onload = resolve; s.onerror = () => reject(new Error('Failed: ' + src))

document.head.appendChild(s)

})

}


// Word limits per tier — proof of concept uses 'free' as default

const FREE_WORD_LIMIT = 10000


function updateAnalyseBtn() {

// Use uploaded file text if present, otherwise textarea

const text = (currentScriptText || document.getElementById('scriptInput').value || '').trim()

const wordCount = text ? text.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length : 0

const has = wordCount > 20

const overLimit = wordCount > FREE_WORD_LIMIT

const btn = document.getElementById('analyseBtn')


// Update word count indicator

const wrap = document.getElementById('wordCountWrap')

if (wrap) wrap.style.display = wordCount > 0 ? 'block' : 'none'

const bar = document.getElementById('wordCountBar')

const num = document.getElementById('wordCountNum')

const lim = document.getElementById('wordCountLimit')

const upg = document.getElementById('wordCountUpgrade')

if (bar && num && lim && upg) {

const pct = Math.min((wordCount / FREE_WORD_LIMIT) * 100, 100)

bar.style.width = pct + '%'

bar.style.background = overLimit ? '#8b2020' : 'var(--amber-d)'

num.textContent = wordCount.toLocaleString() + ' words'

lim.textContent = overLimit ? 'Exceeds free tier limit' : 'of ' + FREE_WORD_LIMIT.toLocaleString() + ' free'

lim.style.color = overLimit ? '#8b2020' : '#3a3830'

if (overLimit) {

upg.style.display = 'block'

upg.textContent = 'Free analysis covers up to 10,000 words — a short film, TV pilot, or short story. For feature-length work, a paid plan is required.'

} else {

upg.style.display = 'none'

}

}


btn.disabled = !has || !apiKey || isStreaming || overLimit

btn.style.background = (has && apiKey && !isStreaming && !overLimit) ? 'var(--amber)' : '#2e2a22'

btn.style.color = (has && apiKey && !isStreaming && !overLimit) ? 'var(--black-band)' : '#5a5448'

btn.style.cursor = (has && apiKey && !isStreaming && !overLimit) ? 'pointer' : 'not-allowed'

}


function showUploadError(msg) {

const el = document.getElementById('uploadError')

el.textContent = msg; el.style.display = 'block'

setTimeout(() => el.style.display = 'none', 6000)

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 9. ANALYSIS & API ══════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── ANALYSIS ────────────────────────────


function detectModeFromText(text) {

const sample = text.slice(0, 1500).toUpperCase()

const scriptMarkers = ['FADE IN', 'EXT.', 'INT.', 'CUT TO', 'DISSOLVE TO', 'SMASH CUT', 'V.O.', '(V.O.)', 'CONTINUED', 'CLOSE ON', 'WIDE ON', 'ANGLE ON']

const playMarkers = ['ACT ONE', 'ACT 1', 'SCENE ONE', 'SCENE 1', 'STAGE DIRECTION', '[LIGHTS', 'LIGHTS UP', 'BLACKOUT', 'CURTAIN']

const scriptScore = scriptMarkers.filter(m => sample.includes(m)).length

const playScore = playMarkers.filter(m => sample.includes(m)).length

if (scriptScore >= 1) return 'script' // 1 marker is enough for a screenplay

if (playScore >= 2) return 'play'

return null // no confident detection

}


async function startAnalysis() {

if (isStreaming) return

// Use uploaded file text if present, otherwise textarea

const text = (currentScriptText || document.getElementById('scriptInput').value || '').trim()

if (!text || !apiKey) return


const genre = document.getElementById('genreSelect').value || 'Auto-detect'

const intent = document.getElementById('intentInput').value.trim()

const bibleIgnore = document.getElementById('bibleIgnore')?.checked || false

const writerBible = (!bibleIgnore && document.getElementById('bibleInput')?.value.trim()) || ''

const wordCount = text.split(/\s+/).length

const pageEst = appMode === 'script' ? Math.round(wordCount / 55) : Math.round(wordCount / 250)


// Auto-detect mode from text content

const detectedMode = detectModeFromText(text)

if (detectedMode && detectedMode !== appMode) setAppMode(detectedMode)


currentScriptText = text

isStreaming = true

currentReport = ''

currentDiagnostic = null

updateAnalyseBtn()

showReportScreen()

showThinking()

setAnalysingMode(true)

// Hide bible pill if skipped

if (bibleIgnore || writerBible) {

const bp = document.getElementById('taskBible')

if (bp) bp.style.display = 'none'

} else {

const bp = document.getElementById('taskBible')

if (bp) bp.style.display = ''

}


// ── Populate title block from real submission ──

const guessedTitle = guessTitle(text) || currentFileName || 'Untitled'

const modeLabel = appMode === 'script' ? 'Script' : appMode === 'story' ? 'Story' : 'Stage play'

const titleEl = document.getElementById('dynTitle')

if (titleEl) titleEl.innerHTML = escHtml(guessedTitle)

// Also set byline from intent if provided

const bylineEl = document.getElementById('dynByline')

if (bylineEl) bylineEl.textContent = intent || ''

setEl('dynKicker', `${modeLabel} analysis · ${genre}`)

setEl('dynByline', intent || '')

setEl('dynSummary', '')

setEl('dynPages', appMode === 'script' ? String(pageEst) : '—')

setEl('dynWords', wordCount.toLocaleString())

setEl('dynGenre', genre)

setEl('dynMode', modeLabel)

setEl('dynVerdictRuling', 'Analysing…')

setEl('dynVerdictDetail', '')

const sep = document.getElementById('dynVerdictSep')

if (sep) sep.style.display = 'none'


// reportContent cleared — will be populated as analysis streams in

document.getElementById('reportContent').innerHTML = ''

// Bible panel — show if not skipped, but body collapsed until generated

const biblePanel = document.getElementById('sec-bible')

if (biblePanel && !bibleIgnore) {

biblePanel.style.display = 'block'

setEl('bibleSourceLabel', writerBible ? 'Writer-provided' : 'Auto-generated')

setEl('bibleContent', writerBible ? writerBible : 'Generating…')

document.getElementById('bibleBody').style.display = 'none'

document.getElementById('bibleChevron').textContent = '▼'

if (writerBible) {

document.getElementById('bibleBody').style.display = 'block'

document.getElementById('bibleChevron').textContent = '▲'

}

} else if (biblePanel) {

biblePanel.style.display = 'none'

}


// ── PASS 1: Deep Read — forms the view before the analysis ───────────────

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Reading your work…')

setEl('dynVerdictRuling', 'Reading…')

let diagnostic = null

try {

diagnostic = await runDeepRead(text, modeLabel)

currentDiagnostic = diagnostic

if (diagnostic.title && diagnostic.title !== 'Untitled') {

const titleEl = document.getElementById('dynTitle')

if (titleEl) titleEl.innerHTML = escHtml(diagnostic.title)

}

if (diagnostic.summary) setEl('dynSummary', diagnostic.summary)

} catch (e) {

console.warn('Pass 1 failed, falling back to single-pass:', e.message)

}


// ── PASS 1b: Combined structure + evidence reader (single call) ──────────

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Reading your work…')

let structuralMap = null

if (diagnostic) {

try {

structuralMap = await runStructuralReader(text, modeLabel, diagnostic)

} catch (e) {

console.warn('Pass 1b failed:', e.message)

}

currentDiagnostic = { ...diagnostic, structuralMap, evidenceMap: structuralMap }

}


// ── PASS 2c: Narrator Verifier — independent verdict before Brain 2 writes ──

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Verifying narrator…')

if (structuralMap?.narratorBehaviour) {

try {

const narratorVerdicts = await runNarratorVerifier(structuralMap)

if (narratorVerdicts && currentDiagnostic) {

currentDiagnostic = { ...currentDiagnostic, narratorVerdicts }

}

} catch(e) { console.warn('Brain 2c failed:', e.message) }

}


setEl('dynVerdictRuling', 'Analysing…')

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Writing analysis…')


// ── PASS 2 + parallel calls — full analysis with diagnostic + map ─────────

await Promise.all([

runMainAnalysis(text, genre, intent, wordCount, pageEst, modeLabel, writerBible, currentDiagnostic),

runDashboardScores(text, genre, wordCount),

runStudioMatching(text, genre, modeLabel, diagnostic?.title || guessedTitle),

...(bibleIgnore || writerBible ? [] : [runBibleGeneration(text, modeLabel)])

])


isStreaming = false

updateAnalyseBtn()

setAnalysingMode(false)

hideThinking()

showConversationPanel()

}


// ── Main analysis — streams sections ─────────────────────────



// ── BRAIN 2c: NARRATOR VERIFIER ────────────────────────────────────────

// Runs before Brain 2. Independently verifies each narrator line.

// Returns locked verdicts that Brain 2 cannot contradict.

async function runNarratorVerifier(structuralMap) {

if (!structuralMap?.narratorBehaviour) return null

const nb = structuralMap.narratorBehaviour

const allLines = [

...(nb.elevating || []).map(q => ({ quote: q, hint: 'elevating' })),

...(nb.worldEstablishment || []).map(q => ({ quote: q, hint: 'world_establishment' })),

...(nb.restating || []).map(q => ({ quote: q, hint: 'restatement' }))

]

if (!allLines.length) return null


const linesText = allLines.map((l,i) => `${i+1}. "${l.quote.substring(0,80)}"`).join('\n')


const system = `You are classifying narrator behaviour in a piece of writing. For each line, classify it as exactly one of:

- ELEVATION: narrator adds something the image/scene cannot show alone (temporal weight, moral dimension, interiority, a feeling with no visual equivalent). This is CORRECT use of narrator.

- WORLD_ESTABLISHMENT: atmospheric description creating the world and tonal register. Never a failure.

- RESTATEMENT: narrator names or explains what the image/scene has already shown completely. This is the only failure.


THE TEST: Could the reader reach this understanding from the image/scene alone, without the narrator? If yes — RESTATEMENT. If no — ELEVATION or WORLD_ESTABLISHMENT.


Return ONLY valid JSON: { "verdicts": [{ "quote": "...", "classification": "ELEVATION|WORLD_ESTABLISHMENT|RESTATEMENT", "reason": "one sentence" }] }`


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({

model: 'claude-opus-4-7',

max_tokens: 1000,

system,

messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Classify these narrator lines:\n\n' + linesText }]

})

})

if (!res.ok) return null

const data = await res.json()

const raw = data.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text || '{}'

return JSON.parse(raw.replace(/```json|```/g, '').trim())

} catch(e) {

console.warn('Brain 2c failed:', e.message)

return null

}

}



// ── BRAIN 2d: NARRATOR CORRECTION PASS (FAILSAFE) ─────────────────────────

// Independent of Brain 2's cooperation. Takes the finished analysis and the

// locked verdicts. Where the analysis criticises a line verified as ELEVATION

// or WORLD_ESTABLISHMENT, it REWRITES that passage to be consistent — it does

// NOT delete it (deletion creates gaps). The surrounding argument is preserved.

async function runNarratorCorrection(analysisText, diagnostic) {

const verdicts = diagnostic?.narratorVerdicts?.verdicts

if (!verdicts || !verdicts.length) return analysisText

const protectedLines = verdicts.filter(v => v.classification !== 'RESTATEMENT')

if (!protectedLines.length) return analysisText


const protectedList = protectedLines

.map(v => '- "' + v.quote.substring(0,70) + '" [' + v.classification + ': ' + v.reason + ']')

.join('\n')


const system = `You are a precise editorial corrector. You will receive a finished editorial analysis and a list of narrator lines that have been INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED as correct narrator usage (ELEVATION = adds what the image cannot carry; WORLD_ESTABLISHMENT = atmosphere/register).


Your ONLY task: find any place in the analysis where these specific verified lines are criticised as failures, restatement, over-writing, "telling not showing", "the image should carry it", or similar. REWRITE only those sentences so they are consistent with the verdict — either acknowledging the line works, or redirecting the note to genuine specificity concerns.


CRITICAL RULES:

- Do NOT delete sentences. Rewrite them so the paragraph stays coherent.

- Do NOT touch any part of the analysis that does not concern these verified lines.

- Do NOT add new notes. Do NOT change the voice or style.

- If the analysis does not criticise any verified line, return it COMPLETELY UNCHANGED.

- Preserve all formatting, headers, and structure exactly.


Return ONLY the corrected analysis. No preamble, no explanation, no markdown fences.


VERIFIED CORRECT NARRATOR LINES (must not be criticised as failures):

${protectedList}`


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({

model: 'claude-opus-4-7',

max_tokens: 6000,

system,

messages: [{ role: 'user', content: analysisText }]

})

})

if (!res.ok) return analysisText

const data = await res.json()

const corrected = data.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text

// Safety: only accept correction if it's substantial (not truncated/empty)

if (corrected && corrected.length > analysisText.length * 0.7) return corrected

return analysisText

} catch(e) {

console.warn('Brain 2d correction failed:', e.message)

return analysisText // fail safe — original analysis survives

}

}


async function runMainAnalysis(text, genre, intent, wordCount, pageEst, modeLabel, bible, diagnostic) {

const systemPrompt = diagnostic

? buildSystemPromptWithDiagnostic(appMode, genre, diagnostic)

: buildSystemPrompt(appMode, genre)

const userPrompt = buildUserPrompt(appMode, text, genre, intent, wordCount, pageEst, bible)

// Prepend Brain 2c narrator verdicts — independently verified, locked

if (currentDiagnostic?.narratorVerdicts?.verdicts?.length) {

const vv = currentDiagnostic.narratorVerdicts.verdicts

const correct = vv.filter(v => v.classification !== 'RESTATEMENT')

.map(v => ' ' + v.classification + ': "' + v.quote.substring(0,60) + '" — ' + v.reason).join('\n')

const failures = vv.filter(v => v.classification === 'RESTATEMENT')

.map(v => ' RESTATEMENT: "' + v.quote.substring(0,60) + '" — ' + v.reason).join('\n')

const verdictBlock = 'NARRATOR VERDICTS — INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED BY BRAIN 2c — THESE ARE FACTS:\n' +

'CORRECT (elevation/world-establishment — DO NOT flag as failures):\n' + (correct || ' none') + '\n' +

'RESTATEMENT (the only actual failure):\n' + (failures || ' none') + '\n\n'

userPrompt = verdictBlock + userPrompt

} else if (currentDiagnostic?.structuralMap?.narratorBehaviour) {

const nb = currentDiagnostic.structuralMap.narratorBehaviour

const el = (nb.elevating||[]).map(q=>' ELEVATING: "'+q.substring(0,60)+'"').join('\n')

const we = (nb.worldEstablishment||[]).map(q=>' WORLD-EST: "'+q.substring(0,60)+'"').join('\n')

const re2 = (nb.restating||[]).map(q=>' RESTATEMENT: "'+q.substring(0,60)+'"').join('\n')

if (el||we||re2) {

userPrompt = 'NARRATOR PRE-CLASSIFICATION:\nCORRECT USE (never flag):\n'+(el||'none')+'\n'+(we||'')+'\nFAILURES:\n'+(re2||'none')+'\n\n' + userPrompt

}

}

const container = document.getElementById('reportContent')

container.innerHTML = ''


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-opus-4-7', max_tokens: 8000, stream: true, thinking: { type: 'adaptive' }, output_config: { effort: 'high' }, system: systemPrompt, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: userPrompt }] })

})

if (!res.ok) { const e = await res.json().catch(()=>({})); throw new Error(e.error?.message || `API ${res.status}`) }

const reader = res.body.getReader()

const dec = new TextDecoder()

let buf = ''

let streamTimeout = setTimeout(() => {

console.warn('Stream timeout — may have stalled')

}, 30000)

while (true) {

const { done, value } = await reader.read()

clearTimeout(streamTimeout)

streamTimeout = setTimeout(() => { console.warn('Stream stalled') }, 15000)

if (done) break

buf += dec.decode(value, { stream: true })

const lines = buf.split('\n'); buf = lines.pop()

for (const line of lines) {

if (!line.startsWith('data: ')) continue

const d = line.slice(6); if (d === '[DONE]') continue

try { const evt = JSON.parse(d); if (evt.type==='content_block_delta' && evt.delta?.type==='text_delta') { currentReport += evt.delta.text; renderLiveReport(currentReport) } } catch {}

}

}

renderLiveReport(currentReport)

// Brain 2d: failsafe narrator correction — rewrites (never deletes) any

// note that flags a verified-elevation line. Independent of Brain 2.

if (currentDiagnostic?.narratorVerdicts) {

setEl('thinkingLabel', 'Final check…')

try {

const corrected = await runNarratorCorrection(currentReport, currentDiagnostic)

if (corrected !== currentReport) {

currentReport = corrected

renderLiveReport(currentReport)

}

} catch(e) { console.warn('Correction pass failed:', e.message) }

}

markTaskDone('taskAnalysis')

} catch (err) {

showErrorBar('Analysis error: ' + err.message)

markTaskDone('taskAnalysis')

}

}


// ── Dashboard scores — JSON call ──────────────────────────────

async function runDashboardScores(text, genre, wordCount) {

const limit = 6000

const excerpt = text.length > limit ? text.slice(0, limit) + '\n[truncated]' : text

const tradition = currentDiagnostic?.tradition || 'unknown'

const prompt = `You are a professional script and story editor. Read the submitted work and score it on two distinct sets of dimensions. Return ONLY a valid JSON object — no other text, no markdown, no backticks.


CONFIRMED TRADITION: ${tradition}


Return exactly this JSON structure:

{"title":"The actual title as it appears in the text. Null if not found.","scores":{"structure":7,"character":5,"dialogue":7,"tone":8,"pace":4,"theme":8},"alignment":{"register":7,"narrator":6,"form":8,"tradition_rules":7,"specificity":6,"earned":7},"beats":[{"pct":10,"intensity":4,"label":"Opening","note":"Brief note"},{"pct":30,"intensity":7,"label":"Inciting Incident","note":"Brief note"},{"pct":50,"intensity":6,"label":"Midpoint","note":"Brief note"},{"pct":75,"intensity":8,"label":"Climax Approach","note":"Brief note"},{"pct":90,"intensity":9,"label":"Resolution","note":"Brief note"}],"summary":"One sentence describing what this work is about."}


SCORES — generic craft quality (1-10): structure, character, dialogue, tone, pace, theme.


ALIGNMENT — how well each element serves the confirmed tradition specifically (1-10):

- register: does the tonal register hold within this tradition?

- narrator: does the narrator/voice earn its role by this tradition's standards?

- form: does the formal approach serve what this tradition requires?

- tradition_rules: are the specific rules of this tradition being followed?

- specificity: do elements retain the human specificity this tradition requires?

- earned: does each formal choice earn its place within the tradition's logic?


These are genuinely different measurements. Alignment scores are tradition-specific.


NARRATIVE STRUCTURE: Before scoring, identify whether this is linear, non-linear, frame narrative, reverse chronology, or multi-timeline. Factor this into your scores — a non-linear work should not be penalised on structure for using flashbacks by design.


SUBMITTED TEXT:

${excerpt}`


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 800, system: 'You are a script and story editor. Return only valid JSON.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }] })

})

if (!res.ok) return

const data = await res.json()

const raw = data.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text || ''

const clean = raw.replace(/```json|```/g, '').trim()

const parsed = JSON.parse(clean)

applyDashboard(parsed)

markTaskDone('taskScores')

} catch (err) {

console.warn('Dashboard scores failed:', err.message)

applyDashboard({ scores: { structure:0, character:0, dialogue:0, tone:0, pace:0, theme:0 }, alignment:{}, beats:[], summary:'' })

markTaskDone('taskScores')

}

}


function applyDashboard(data) {

const { scores, alignment, beats, summary, title } = data


// Update title if AI found a real one — overrides guessTitle

if (title) {

const titleEl = document.getElementById('dynTitle')

if (titleEl) titleEl.innerHTML = escHtml(title)

}


if (summary) setEl('dynSummary', summary)


// ── Tradition alignment bars (what the bar grid now shows) ────────────

const alignDims = [

{ key: 'register', label: 'Register' },

{ key: 'narrator', label: 'Narrator/Voice' },

{ key: 'form', label: 'Form' },

{ key: 'tradition_rules', label: 'Tradition Rules' },

{ key: 'specificity', label: 'Specificity' },

{ key: 'earned', label: 'Earned Weight' },

]

const grid = document.getElementById('scoreBarGrid')

if (grid && alignment) {

grid.innerHTML = alignDims.map(d => {

const s = (alignment[d.key]) || 0

const pct = s * 10

const col = s >= 7 ? 'var(--teal)' : s >= 5 ? 'var(--amber)' : 'var(--tension)'

return `<div class="bar-item">

<div class="bar-label-row"><span class="bar-label">${d.label}</span><span class="bar-score">${s}</span></div>

<div class="bar-track"><div class="bar-fill" style="width:${pct}%;background:${col}"></div></div>

</div>`

}).join('')

}


// ── Radar shape (generic craft balance) ──────────────────────────────

const dims = [

{ key: 'structure', label: 'Structure' },

{ key: 'character', label: 'Character' },

{ key: 'dialogue', label: 'Dialogue' },

{ key: 'tone', label: 'Tone' },

{ key: 'pace', label: 'Pace' },

{ key: 'theme', label: 'Theme' },

]

const shape = document.getElementById('radarShape')

if (shape && scores) {

// Centre 110,108. Full radius to outer polygon vertex ~85px

const vals = [

(scores.structure || 5) / 10,

(scores.character || 5) / 10,

(scores.dialogue || 5) / 10,

(scores.tone || 5) / 10,

(scores.pace || 5) / 10,

(scores.theme || 5) / 10,

]

// Outer vertices of hexagon (from SVG grid)

const outerPts = [

[110, 18], // top — structure

[187, 63], // top-right — character

[187, 153], // bottom-right — dialogue

[110, 198], // bottom — tone

[33, 153], // bottom-left — pace

[33, 63], // top-left — theme

]

const cx = 110, cy = 108

const pts = vals.map((v, i) => {

const ox = outerPts[i][0], oy = outerPts[i][1]

const x = cx + (ox - cx) * v

const y = cy + (oy - cy) * v

return `${x.toFixed(1)},${y.toFixed(1)}`

})

shape.setAttribute('points', pts.join(' '))

}


// ── Arc beats ────────────────────────────

const arcBeats = document.getElementById('arcBeats')

if (arcBeats && beats && beats.length > 0) {

const svgW = 860, svgH = 180

let tensionPts = '', pacePts = '', emotionPts = ''


// Build smooth lines from beats

const addedPts = beats.map(b => {

const x = (b.pct / 100) * svgW

const intensity = b.intensity || 5

const y = svgH - (intensity / 10) * (svgH - 20) - 10

return { x, y, label: b.label, note: b.note }

})


// Prefix with start point

const allPts = [{ x: 0, y: 90, label: '', note: '' }, ...addedPts]

const ptStr = allPts.map(p => `${p.x.toFixed(0)},${p.y.toFixed(0)}`).join(' ')


// All three lines use same beat points with slight offsets for visual separation

document.getElementById('lineTension').setAttribute('points',

allPts.map((p,i) => `${p.x.toFixed(0)},${Math.max(10, Math.min(170, p.y - 8)).toFixed(0)}`).join(' '))

document.getElementById('linePace').setAttribute('points',

allPts.map((p,i) => `${p.x.toFixed(0)},${Math.max(10, Math.min(170, p.y + 4)).toFixed(0)}`).join(' '))

document.getElementById('lineEmotion').setAttribute('points',

allPts.map((p,i) => `${p.x.toFixed(0)},${Math.max(10, Math.min(170, p.y - 2)).toFixed(0)}`).join(' '))


// Beat markers

arcBeats.innerHTML = addedPts.map(p => `

<g class="beat-group" data-label="${escAttr(p.label)}" data-note="${escAttr(p.note)}" data-cx="${p.x.toFixed(0)}" data-cy="${p.y.toFixed(0)}">

<g transform="translate(${(p.x - 8).toFixed(0)},${(p.y - 31).toFixed(0)})" opacity=".7">

<path d="M8,2 L8,12 M1,1 L1,13 Q4.5,12 8,13 Q11.5,12 15,13 L15,1 Q11.5,2 8,1 Q4.5,2 1,1Z" stroke="var(--ink-soft)" stroke-width=".9" fill="none" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

</g>

<circle cx="${p.x.toFixed(0)}" cy="${p.y.toFixed(0)}" r="5" fill="var(--paper)" stroke="var(--amber)" stroke-width="1.5" style="cursor:pointer"/>

</g>`).join('')


// Re-attach tooltip listeners

arcBeats.querySelectorAll('.beat-group').forEach(g => {

const circle = g.querySelector('circle')

if (!circle) return

circle.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {

const cx = parseFloat(g.dataset.cx), pct = cx / 860

document.getElementById('tooltipLabel').textContent = g.dataset.label

document.getElementById('tooltipNote').textContent = g.dataset.note

document.getElementById('arcTooltip').style.left = (pct * 100) + '%'

document.getElementById('arcTooltip').classList.add('visible')

})

circle.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => document.getElementById('arcTooltip').classList.remove('visible'))

})

}

}


// ── Studio matching — generates real recommendations ─────────

async function runStudioMatching(text, genre, modeLabel, title) {

const limit = 4000

const excerpt = text.length > limit ? text.slice(0, limit) + '\n[truncated]' : text

const prompt = `You are a literary and film industry expert. Based on the submitted ${modeLabel}, identify the 4 to 6 best-matched studios, publishers, or streaming platforms that would be most likely to develop or acquire this specific work.


For each, provide:

- name: the studio/publisher name

- type: their type (e.g. "Indie Studio · Theatrical", "Literary Publisher", "Streaming Original")

- match: one of "Strong Match", "Good Match", "Possible Match"

- rationale: 2-3 sentences explaining WHY this specific work fits this specific company — reference the work's actual content, tone, and market position

- contact: brief contact/submission info with website URL if known


Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no backticks:

{"studios":[{"name":"...","type":"...","match":"...","rationale":"...","contact":"..."},...]}


SUBMITTED TEXT:

${excerpt}`


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 1200, system: 'You are a film and literary industry expert. Return only valid JSON.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }] })

})

if (!res.ok) return

const data = await res.json()

const raw = data.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text || ''

const clean = raw.replace(/```json|```/g, '').trim()

const parsed = JSON.parse(clean)

applyStudios(parsed.studios || [])

markTaskDone('taskStudios')

} catch (err) {

console.warn('Studio matching failed:', err.message)

document.getElementById('studioGrid').innerHTML = '<div style="grid-column:1/-1;padding:2rem;font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.65rem;letter-spacing:.1em;color:var(--ink-soft)">Studio matching unavailable — please try again.</div>'

markTaskDone('taskStudios')

}

}


function applyStudios(studios) {

const grid = document.getElementById('studioGrid')

if (!grid || !studios.length) return

const matchColour = m => m === 'Strong Match' ? 'rgba(42,80,48,.3)' : m === 'Good Match' ? 'rgba(168,108,16,.2)' : 'rgba(74,72,64,.2)'

const matchBorder = m => m === 'Strong Match' ? 'rgba(42,80,48,.5)' : m === 'Good Match' ? 'rgba(168,108,16,.4)' : 'rgba(74,72,64,.3)'

const matchText = m => m === 'Strong Match' ? '#4a9a60' : m === 'Good Match' ? 'var(--amber-l)' : '#9a9080'

grid.innerHTML = studios.map(s => `

<div class="studio-card">

<div class="studio-card-header">

<div><div class="studio-name">${escHtml(s.name)}</div><div class="studio-type">${escHtml(s.type)}</div></div>

<div class="studio-match" style="background:${matchColour(s.match)};color:${matchText(s.match)};border-color:${matchBorder(s.match)}">${escHtml(s.match)}</div>

</div>

<div class="studio-rationale">${escHtml(s.rationale)}</div>

<div class="studio-contact">${escHtml(s.contact)}</div>

</div>`).join('')

}


// ── Bible generation ──────────────────────────────────────────

async function runBibleGeneration(text, modeLabel) {

const limit = 8000

const excerpt = text.length > limit ? text.slice(0, limit) + '\n[truncated]' : text


const prompt = `Read the submitted ${modeLabel} and extract a character bible. For every named character, record:

- Name and role

- Physical description (only what is explicitly stated)

- Established personality traits and behaviour patterns

- Speech register and verbal habits

- Stated wants and apparent needs

- Key relationships

- Any backstory explicitly given

- Any contradictions or inconsistencies already present in the text


Be factual. Only record what is explicitly present in the text. Do not infer or invent. If something is ambiguous, mark it as ambiguous.


Format as plain text, one character per section, clearly separated.


SUBMITTED TEXT:

${excerpt}`


try {

const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01', 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },

body: JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 1200, system: 'You are a script editor building a character bible. Record only what is explicitly in the text. No fabrication.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }] })

})

if (!res.ok) return

const data = await res.json()

const bibleText = data.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text || ''

setEl('bibleContent', bibleText)

markTaskDone('taskBible')

} catch (err) {

setEl('bibleContent', 'Bible generation failed. You can paste your own bible before the next analysis.')

markTaskDone('taskBible')

}

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 10. RENDERING ══════════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── LIVE REPORT RENDERER ────────────────

function renderLiveReport(text) {

try {

const container = document.getElementById('reportContent')

if (!container) return

if (!text || !text.trim()) return

container.innerHTML = ''


// Extract verdict and push into the dedicated verdict band

const vm = text.match(/VERDICT:\s*\[?([^\]\n\[]{3,60})\]?\n?([\s\S]{0,400}?)(?=\n##|\n---|$)/i)

if (vm) {

const vtext = vm[1].replace(/[\[\]]/g,'').trim()

const vdetail = vm[2].replace(/\[.*?\]/g,'').trim()

const verdictColorValue = verdictColour(vtext)

setEl('dynVerdictRuling', vtext)

setEl('dynVerdictDetail', vdetail)

const acc = document.getElementById('dynVerdictAccent')

if (acc) acc.style.background = verdictColorValue

const rul = document.getElementById('dynVerdictRuling')

if (rul) rul.style.color = verdictColorValue

const sep = document.getElementById('dynVerdictSep')

if (sep) sep.style.display = vdetail ? 'block' : 'none'

}


// Parse sections — extract CRAFT DIRECTIVES separately

const sections = []

let craftDirectives = null

let whereToBegin = null

let actionPlan = null

const lines = text.split('\n')

let cur = null

for (const line of lines) {

const hm = line.match(/^##\s+(.+)/)

if (hm) {

if (cur) {

if (cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('CRAFT DIRECTIVE') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('WHAT TO FIX')) craftDirectives = cur

else if (cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('WHERE TO BEGIN') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('NEXT STEP') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('WHERE TO START')) whereToBegin = cur

else if (cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('ACTION PLAN') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('REVISION PLAN') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('STEP-BY-STEP') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('NEXT STEPS')) actionPlan = cur

else sections.push(cur)

}

cur = { h: hm[1].trim(), b: '' }

}

else if (line.match(/^---+$/) || line.match(/^DRAFT/)) {}

else if (cur) cur.b += line + '\n'

}

if (cur) {

if (cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('CRAFT DIRECTIVE') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('WHAT TO FIX')) craftDirectives = cur

else if (cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('WHERE TO BEGIN') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('NEXT STEP') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('WHERE TO START')) whereToBegin = cur

else if (cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('ACTION PLAN') || cur.h.toUpperCase().includes('REVISION PLAN')) actionPlan = cur

else sections.push(cur)

}


const tinted = [false,true,false,true,false,true,false,true,false,true,false,true]

sections.forEach((sec, i) => {

if (!sec.b.trim()) return

const isOpen = i < 5

const bg = tinted[i % tinted.length] ? 'var(--cream)' : 'transparent'

const margin = tinted[i % tinted.length] ? '0 -3rem' : '0'

const padding = tinted[i % tinted.length] ? '0 3rem' : '0'

const id = 'sec-' + String(i + 1).padStart(2, '0')

container.insertAdjacentHTML('beforeend', `

<div id="${id}" style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--rule-l);background:${bg};margin:${margin};padding:${padding}">

<button onclick="toggleLiveSec('${id}')" style="width:100%;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;padding:1.25rem 0;background:none;border:none;cursor:pointer;text-align:left">

<span style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:1rem">

<span style="font-family:'Libre Baskerville',serif;font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:var(--rule-l);width:2.5rem">${String(i+1).padStart(2,'0')}</span>

<span style="font-family:'Libre Baskerville',serif;font-size:.85rem;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:.01em;text-transform:none;color:var(--ink)">${escHtml(sec.h)}</span>

</span>

<span class="sec-chev-${id}" style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.7rem;color:var(--rule)">${isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}</span>

</button>

<div class="sec-body-${id}" style="display:${isOpen ? 'block' : 'none'};padding-bottom:1.75rem">

${formatBody(sec.b.trim())}

</div>

</div>`)

})


// Streaming cursor

if (isStreaming && sections.length === 0 && text) {

container.insertAdjacentHTML('beforeend', `<p style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.8rem;color:var(--ink-soft);white-space:pre-wrap;line-height:1.8">${escHtml(text)}<span style="display:inline-block;width:2px;height:1em;background:var(--amber);vertical-align:text-bottom;animation:blink 1s ease-in-out infinite;margin-left:2px"></span></p>`)

}


// WHERE TO BEGIN — distinct section with next-steps register

if (whereToBegin && whereToBegin.b.trim()) {

container.insertAdjacentHTML('beforeend', `

<div style="margin-top:2rem;background:var(--black-band);padding:2rem;border-left:3px solid var(--teal)">

<div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.72rem;letter-spacing:.2em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--teal);margin-bottom:1rem">Where To Begin</div>

<div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Sans',sans-serif;font-size:.9rem;line-height:1.8;color:#f0ead8">${formatBody(whereToBegin.b.trim())}</div>

</div>`)

}


// ACTION PLAN — step by step revision instructions

if (actionPlan && actionPlan.b.trim()) {

container.insertAdjacentHTML('beforeend', `

<div id="sec-action" style="margin-top:2rem;background:var(--black-band);padding:2rem;border-left:3px solid var(--amber);text-align:left">

<div style="font-family:'Libre Baskerville',serif;font-size:1rem;font-weight:700;color:var(--amber-l);margin-bottom:1rem;letter-spacing:0">Action Plan</div>

<div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Sans',sans-serif;font-size:.88rem;line-height:1.85;color:#f0ead8">${formatBody(actionPlan.b.trim())}</div>

</div>`)

}


// Craft directives — rendered as a compact numbered list after sections

if (craftDirectives && craftDirectives.b.trim()) {

const dirLines = craftDirectives.b.trim().split('\n')

.map(l => l.trim())

.filter(l => l && /^\d+\./.test(l))

if (dirLines.length > 0) {

container.insertAdjacentHTML('beforeend', `

<div style="margin-top:2rem;background:var(--black-band);padding:1.75rem 2rem;border-left:3px solid var(--amber-d)">

<div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.72rem;letter-spacing:.2em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--amber);margin-bottom:1.25rem">What To Fix Next</div>

<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:.5rem">

${dirLines.map((line, i) => {

const directive = line.replace(/^\d+\.\s*/, '')

return `<div style="display:flex;gap:1rem;align-items:baseline">

<span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.6rem;color:var(--amber-d);min-width:1.5rem;flex-shrink:0">${String(i+1).padStart(2,'0')}</span>

<span style="font-size:.88rem;line-height:1.6;color:var(--ink-soft)">${escHtml(directive)}</span>

</div>`

}).join('')}

</div>

</div>`)

}

}

} catch(e) { console.warn('renderLiveReport error:', e.message) }

}


function toggleLiveSec(id) {

const body = document.querySelector('.sec-body-' + id)

const chev = document.querySelector('.sec-chev-' + id)

if (!body) return

const open = body.style.display !== 'none'

body.style.display = open ? 'none' : 'block'

if (chev) chev.textContent = open ? '▼' : '▲'

}


function verdictColour(v) {

v = v.toUpperCase()

if (v.includes('RECOMMEND') || v.includes('PUBLISH')) return '#2a7a4a'

if (v.includes('CONSIDER') || v.includes('DEVELOP')) return 'var(--amber)'

if (v.includes('WATCH')) return '#2a7a7a'

return '#8b2020'

}


function formatBody(text) {

text = text.replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '<strong style="color:var(--ink);font-weight:500">$1</strong>')

text = text.replace(/\*([^*\n]+?)\*/g, '<em style="color:var(--ink-soft)">$1</em>')

text = text.replace(/^>\s*(.+)$/gm, `<div style="margin:1rem 0;padding:.85rem 1.1rem;background:var(--warm-mid);border-left:2px solid var(--amber);font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:.78rem;line-height:1.7;color:var(--ink-soft);max-width:600px">$1</div>`)

const paras = text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(p => p.trim())

return paras.map(p => {

p = p.trim()

if (p.startsWith('<div')) return p

return `<p style="font-size:.92rem;line-height:1.88;color:var(--ink-mid);margin-bottom:.85rem;max-width:660px">${p.replace(/\n/g,' ')}</p>`

}).join('')

}


function escHtml(s) {

return String(s).replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;').replace(/"/g,'&quot;')

}


function escAttr(s) {

return String(s).replace(/"/g,'&quot;').replace(/'/g,'&#39;')

}


function setEl(id, text) {

const el = document.getElementById(id)

if (el) el.textContent = text

}


function guessTitle(text) {

const lines = text.split('\n').map(l => l.trim()).filter(Boolean)


// Skip known noise patterns at the top of PDFs/scripts

const skipPatterns = [

/^%PDF/i, /^endobj/i, /^stream/i, /^xref/i,

/^<\//, /^\d+\s+\d+\s+obj/,

/^FADE IN/i, /^INT\./i, /^EXT\./i,

/^Written by/i, /^Screenplay by/i, /^Draft/i,

/^Page \d/i, /^\d+$/, /^www\./i, /^http/i,

/^©/, /^Copyright/i, /^All rights/i,

/^TITLE PAGE/i, /^WGA/i,

]


for (const line of lines.slice(0, 30)) {

// Must be plausible as a title: reasonable length, mostly printable chars

if (line.length < 2 || line.length > 80) continue

if (skipPatterns.some(p => p.test(line))) continue

// Skip lines that are mostly non-ASCII (PDF binary noise)

const printable = line.split('').filter(c => c.charCodeAt(0) > 31 && c.charCodeAt(0) < 127).length

if (printable / line.length < 0.8) continue

// Skip lines that look like metadata key:value

if (/^[A-Z][a-z]+:\s/.test(line)) continue

// Looks good

return line.replace(/["""'']/g, '').trim()

}

return null

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 11. PROMPTS ════════════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── SYSTEM + USER PROMPTS ───────────────

// ── BRAIN 1b — STRUCTURAL READER ─────────────────────────────────────────

// Second pass: given confirmed tradition, map the evidence.

// Returns structured findings for Brain 2 to evaluate rather than discover.

// ── BRAIN 1b: COMBINED STRUCTURE + EVIDENCE READER ──────────────────────

const STRUCTURAL_READER_SYSTEM = `You are a close reader. Tradition and register are confirmed. Your ONLY job is to map and collect evidence. Do NOT evaluate. Do NOT give notes. Return ONLY valid JSON.


{

"narrativeStructure": "linear | non-linear | frame narrative | reverse chronology | multi-timeline — be specific",

"timelineNotes": "How time actually works in this text. If non-linear: opening temporal position and where it jumps.",

"structuralBeats": ["opening position", "first major shift", "key structural moment", "climax", "resolution — quote or describe each"],

"registerMap": [

{"position": "opening|early|mid|late|closing", "quote": "exact short quote from text", "register": "mythic|realist|gothic|generic|mixed|fractured", "note": "one line on what this register is doing"}

],

"strongest": ["exact quote — writing at its best, register fully held"],

"weakest": ["exact quote — where register drops, loses confidence, or becomes generic"],

"registerFractures": ["exact quote where formal register breaks without apparent justification"],

"narratorBehaviour": {

"elevating": ["exact quote — narrator adds dimension image cannot carry alone"],

"restating": ["exact quote — narrator names or explains what image already showed"],

"worldEstablishment": ["exact quote — narrator establishing atmosphere/register, not allegory"]

},

"juxtapositions": ["describe each deliberate contrast — past/present, mythic/contemporary — and whether each side is specific or generic"],

"characterMap": ["character name — role, key moment, consistency"]

}


Return ONLY the JSON. No preamble. No markdown. No evaluation.`


async function runDeepRead(text, modeLabel) {

// Send opening + closing of text for tradition identification

// This is fast, cheap, and forms the view that guides everything else

const maxChars = 3000

const opening = text.slice(0, maxChars)

const closing = text.length > maxChars * 2 ? text.slice(-maxChars) : ''

const excerpt = closing

? `[OPENING OF WORK]\n${opening}\n\n[CLOSING OF WORK]\n${closing}`

: opening


const res = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {

method: 'POST',

headers: {

'Content-Type': 'application/json',

'x-api-key': apiKey,

'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01',

'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true'

},

body: JSON.stringify({

model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',

max_tokens: 800,

system: PASS1_SYSTEM,

messages: [{

role: 'user',

content: `This is a ${modeLabel}. Read carefully and return the diagnostic JSON.\n\n${excerpt}`

}]

})

})


if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Pass 1 failed: ${res.status}`)

const data = await res.json()

const raw = data.content?.find(b => b.type === 'text')?.text || '{}'

try {

return JSON.parse(raw.replace(/```json|```/g, '').trim())

} catch {

return { tradition: 'Unknown', register: 'Unknown', ambition: '', craftQuestions: [], strengths: [], primaryConcern: '', title: 'Untitled', summary: '', formNotes: '' }

}

}


function buildSystemPrompt(mode, genre) {

const base = mode === 'script' ? SCRIPT_SYSTEM : mode === 'story' ? STORY_SYSTEM : PLAY_SYSTEM

const gNote = mode === 'script' ? (SCRIPT_GENRE_NOTES[genre] || SCRIPT_GENRE_NOTES['Auto-detect'])

: mode === 'story' ? (STORY_GENRE_NOTES[genre] || STORY_GENRE_NOTES['Auto-detect'])

: (PLAY_GENRE_NOTES[genre] || PLAY_GENRE_NOTES['Auto-detect'])

return base + '\n\nGENRE / FORM CONTEXT:\n' + gNote

}


function buildSystemPromptWithDiagnostic(mode, genre, diagnostic) {

const base = buildSystemPrompt(mode, genre)

if (!diagnostic || !diagnostic.tradition) return base


const diagnosticBlock = `


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ESTABLISHED DIAGNOSTIC — READ BEFORE EVALUATING

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━


This work has already been read. The following is established fact — do not re-identify, do not override.


TRADITION: ${diagnostic.tradition}

REGISTER: ${diagnostic.register}

AMBITION: ${diagnostic.ambition}

FORM NOTES: ${diagnostic.formNotes}


GENUINE STRENGTHS (already identified — these must be named in your analysis):

${(diagnostic.strengths || []).map((s, i) => `${i+1}. ${s}`).join('\n')}


PRIMARY CONCERN (this must be addressed first and most fully):

${diagnostic.primaryConcern}


CRAFT QUESTIONS THIS ANALYSIS MUST ANSWER:

${(diagnostic.craftQuestions || []).map((q, i) => `${i+1}. ${q}`).join('\n')}


${(function(){

if (!diagnostic || !diagnostic.structuralMap) return ''

var sm = diagnostic.structuralMap

var lines = [

'',

'━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',

'STRUCTURAL MAP — EVIDENCE FROM CLOSE READING (BRAIN 1b)',

'━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',

'This is established evidence. Do not contradict without specific justification.',

'',

'STRUCTURE: ' + (sm.narrativeStructure || 'not determined'),

]

if (sm.timelineNotes) lines.push('TIMELINE: ' + sm.timelineNotes)

if (sm.structuralBeats && sm.structuralBeats.length) lines.push('BEATS: ' + sm.structuralBeats.join(' → '))

if (sm.registerMap && sm.registerMap.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('REGISTER MAP:')

sm.registerMap.forEach(function(r){ lines.push(' ' + r.position + ': "' + r.quote + '" [' + r.register + '] — ' + r.note) })

}

if (sm.strongest && sm.strongest.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('STRONGEST MOMENTS (name these as genuine strengths):')

sm.strongest.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

if (sm.weakest && sm.weakest.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('WEAKEST MOMENTS (address specifically):')

sm.weakest.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

if (sm.registerFractures && sm.registerFractures.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('REGISTER FRACTURES:')

sm.registerFractures.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

var nb = sm.narratorBehaviour || {}

if (nb.elevating && nb.elevating.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('NARRATOR ELEVATING (correct — add dimension image cannot carry):')

nb.elevating.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

if (nb.restating && nb.restating.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('NARRATOR RESTATING (flag these — narrator explains what image showed):')

nb.restating.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

if (nb.worldEstablishment && nb.worldEstablishment.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('NARRATOR WORLD-ESTABLISHMENT (never flag — atmosphere creation):')

nb.worldEstablishment.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

if (sm.juxtapositions && sm.juxtapositions.length) {

lines.push('')

lines.push('JUXTAPOSITIONS:')

sm.juxtapositions.forEach(function(s){ lines.push(' — ' + s) })

}

return lines.join('\n')

})()}


You are now evaluating this work on its own terms within its confirmed tradition. Apply ONLY the craft principles appropriate to ${diagnostic.tradition}. Do not apply principles from a different tradition.\n\nINTENTIONAL JUXTAPOSITION AND REGISTER SPECIFICITY — MANDATORY:


Before critiquing any montage, structural contrast, or tonal shift — identify:

1. Is this intentional juxtaposition? (past/present, mythic/contemporary, specific/abstract)

2. If yes — is the juxtaposition earning its effect? Does it deepen what came before?

3. REGISTER SPECIFICITY: When contemporary material (news clips, social media, YouTube) appears alongside mythic/gothic material — the craft question is not "should this exist?" but "is the contemporary material specific enough to inhabit the same moral world as the mythic material?" Generic news-speak ("we grew fat on greed") lacks the specificity of the surrounding world. A single shot of a specific mundane act — one worker, one action, one moment — can carry what an abstract voiceover cannot. The problem is not the juxtaposition; it may be the specificity of the contemporary material.


INTENTIONAL JUXTAPOSITION — MANDATORY AWARENESS: Before critiquing any montage, structural contrast, or tonal shift — consider whether it is an intentional juxtaposition. The writer may be deliberately placing abstract or rhetorical material next to specific embodied material to create meaning through contrast. If so, the note is not 'remove this' but 'is the juxtaposition earning its effect?' Ask: does the contrast deepen what came before, or dilute it? Does the abstract material add something the specific cannot carry alone — or does it explain what the specific has already made felt? Explore both possibilities before concluding.


NARRATIVE STRUCTURE — MANDATORY BEFORE ANY NOTE: Identify what narrative structure this work uses before giving any note about missing backstory, absent character development, or incomplete emotional preparation. Non-linear structures, frame narratives, reverse chronologies, and multi-timeline works deliver these elements out of sequential order by design. A script that opens with the transformed figure and then flashes back 264 years to show human origins HAS provided the backstory. Confirm the structure — then evaluate whether it does its job with sufficient specificity and weight.`


return base + '\n\nGENRE / FORM CONTEXT:\n' + (mode === 'script' ? (SCRIPT_GENRE_NOTES[genre] || SCRIPT_GENRE_NOTES['Auto-detect']) : mode === 'story' ? (STORY_GENRE_NOTES[genre] || STORY_GENRE_NOTES['Auto-detect']) : (PLAY_GENRE_NOTES[genre] || PLAY_GENRE_NOTES['Auto-detect'])) + diagnosticBlock

}


function buildUserPrompt(mode, text, genre, intent, wordCount, pageEst, bible) {

const intentBlock = intent ? `WRITER'S STATED INTENT: "${intent}"\nIf the work is failing to achieve this, that is the most important note.\n\n` : ''

const bibleBlock = bible ? `CHARACTER BIBLE PROVIDED BY WRITER:\n${bible}\nCross-reference all character analysis against this. Flag any deviations found in the text.\n\n` : ''

const meta = mode === 'script' ? `SCRIPT | Est. ${pageEst} pages | Genre: ${genre}`

: mode === 'story' ? `STORY | ${wordCount} words | Form: ${genre}`

: `STAGE PLAY | ${wordCount} words | Form: ${genre}`

const struct = mode === 'script' ? SCRIPT_REPORT_STRUCTURE : mode === 'story' ? STORY_REPORT_STRUCTURE : SCRIPT_REPORT_STRUCTURE

const limit = 14000

const truncated = text.length > limit ? text.slice(0, limit) + '\n\n[Text truncated at 14,000 characters — analyse what is present]' : text

return `${intentBlock}${bibleBlock}${meta}\n\nProduce the full analysis report using the exact section structure below. Every section must be specific, direct, and quote from the submitted text where possible. Do not fabricate page numbers or scenes that are not present.\n\n${struct}\n\n---\nSUBMITTED TEXT:\n${truncated}`

}



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 12. DOWNLOAD & COPY ════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── DOWNLOAD / COPY ─────────────────

function downloadReport() {

const title = document.getElementById('dynTitle')?.textContent?.trim() || 'Untitled'

const verdict = document.getElementById('dynVerdictRuling')?.textContent?.trim() || ''

const verdictDetail = document.getElementById('dynVerdictDetail')?.textContent?.trim() || ''

const summary = document.getElementById('dynSummary')?.textContent?.trim() || ''


let out = []

out.push('DRAFT & LENS — EDITORIAL ANALYSIS')

out.push('═'.repeat(50))

out.push('')

out.push(title.toUpperCase())

if (summary) out.push(summary)

out.push('')


if (verdict) {

out.push('VERDICT')

out.push('─'.repeat(30))

out.push(verdict)

if (verdictDetail) out.push(verdictDetail)

out.push('')

}


// All analysis sections from reportContent — expanded text only

const reportEl = document.getElementById('reportContent')

if (reportEl) {

// Get all section divs

const sectionDivs = reportEl.querySelectorAll('[id^="sec-"]')

sectionDivs.forEach(div => {

// Get section heading

const heading = div.querySelector('button span:nth-child(2) span:last-child')

const body = div.querySelector('[class^="sec-body-"]')

if (heading && body) {

out.push(heading.textContent.trim().toUpperCase())

out.push('─'.repeat(30))

out.push(body.innerText.trim())

out.push('')

}

})

// Craft directives

const directives = reportEl.querySelector('#sec-directives')

if (directives) {

out.push('WHAT TO FIX NEXT')

out.push('─'.repeat(30))

out.push(directives.innerText.replace('What To Fix Next', '').trim())

out.push('')

}

// Fallback: if no structured sections, grab all text

if (sectionDivs.length === 0) {

out.push('ANALYSIS')

out.push('─'.repeat(30))

out.push(reportEl.innerText.trim())

out.push('')

}

}


// Character bible

const bibleContent = document.getElementById('bibleContent')?.textContent?.trim()

if (bibleContent && bibleContent !== 'Generating…') {

out.push('CHARACTER BIBLE')

out.push('─'.repeat(30))

out.push(bibleContent)

out.push('')

}


out.push('─'.repeat(50))

out.push('Generated by Draft & Lens — draftandlens.com')


const blob = new Blob([out.join('\n')], { type: 'text/plain;charset=utf-8' })

const a = document.createElement('a')

a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob)

a.download = `DraftLens_${title.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, '_').slice(0, 40)}.txt`

document.body.appendChild(a)

a.click()

document.body.removeChild(a)

URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href)

}


function copyReport() {

const title = document.getElementById('dynTitle')?.textContent?.trim() || 'Untitled'

const reportEl = document.getElementById('reportContent')

const text = (document.getElementById('dynVerdictRuling')?.textContent || '') + '\n\n' +

(reportEl?.innerText || '')

navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)

.then(() => alert('Analysis copied to clipboard.'))

.catch(() => alert('Copy failed — please select and copy manually.'))

}



// ═══════════════════════════════════════════

// FUNCTIONAL ENGINE

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 13. UI COMPONENTS ══════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── ARC LINE TOGGLE ─────────────────

function toggleLine(el, name) {

const line = document.getElementById('line' + name.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + name.slice(1))

if (!line) return

const hidden = line.style.display === 'none'

line.style.display = hidden ? '' : 'none'

el.classList.toggle('muted', !hidden)

}


// ─── ARC BEAT TOOLTIPS ───────────────

const tooltip = document.getElementById('arcTooltip')

document.querySelectorAll('.beat-group').forEach(g => {

const circle = g.querySelector('circle')

if (!circle) return

circle.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {

const cx = parseFloat(g.dataset.cx)

const pct = cx / 860

document.getElementById('tooltipLabel').textContent = g.dataset.label

document.getElementById('tooltipNote').textContent = g.dataset.note

tooltip.style.left = (pct * 100) + '%'

tooltip.classList.add('visible')

})

circle.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => tooltip.classList.remove('visible'))

})


// ─── COLLAPSIBLE SECTIONS ────────────

function toggleSection(id) {

const wrap = document.getElementById(id)

if (!wrap) return

const isOpen = wrap.classList.contains('open')

wrap.classList.toggle('open', !isOpen)

const chevron = wrap.querySelector('.section-chevron')

if (chevron) chevron.textContent = isOpen ? '▼' : '▲'

// update sidebar

updateSidebarActive()

}


// ─── SIDEBAR SCROLL TRACKING ─────────

function updateSidebarActive() {

const links = document.querySelectorAll('.sidebar-link')

const sections = []

links.forEach(link => {

const href = link.getAttribute('href')

if (href && href.startsWith('#')) {

const el = document.querySelector(href)

if (el) sections.push({ link, el })

}

})

const scrollY = window.scrollY + 80

let active = sections[0]

sections.forEach(s => {

if (s.el.offsetTop <= scrollY) active = s

})

links.forEach(l => l.classList.remove('active'))

if (active) active.link.classList.add('active')

}

window.addEventListener('scroll', updateSidebarActive)

updateSidebarActive()



// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 14. RATING ═════════════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════

let currentLensRating = 0


function hoverStars(n) {

document.querySelectorAll('.star').forEach(s => {

s.style.color = parseInt(s.dataset.v) <= n ? 'var(--amber)' : 'var(--rule-l)'

})

}

function resetStars() {

document.querySelectorAll('.star').forEach(s => {

s.style.color = parseInt(s.dataset.v) <= currentLensRating ? 'var(--amber)' : 'var(--rule-l)'

})

}

function rateLens(n) {

currentLensRating = n

const labels = ['', 'Not useful', 'Slightly useful', 'Useful', 'Very useful', 'Indispensable']

resetStars()

setEl('ratingLabel', labels[n])

}


function showLensRating() {

const r = document.getElementById('lensRating')

if (r) r.style.display = 'flex'

currentLensRating = 0

resetStars()

setEl('ratingLabel', '')

}


function toggleBiblePanel() {

const body = document.getElementById('bibleBody')

const chev = document.getElementById('bibleChevron')

if (!body) return

const open = body.style.display !== 'none'

body.style.display = open ? 'none' : 'block'

if (chev) chev.textContent = open ? '▼' : '▲'

}


function showErrorBar(msg) {

const bar = document.getElementById('errorBar')

if (bar) { bar.textContent = msg; bar.style.display = 'block'; setTimeout(() => bar.style.display = 'none', 8000) }

}




// ════════════════════════════════════════

// ═══ 15. CONVERSATION ═══════════════════

// ════════════════════════════════════════


function showConversationPanel() {

const panel = document.getElementById('conversationPanel')

if (!panel) return

panel.style.display = 'block'

buildConvSuggestions()

buildConvLensTargets()

}


function setConvTarget(id) {

convTarget = id

// Update editorial button

const editBtn = document.getElementById('convTargetEditorial')

if (editBtn) {

editBtn.className = id === 'editorial' ? '' : 'conv-target-btn'

editBtn.style.cssText = id === 'editorial'

? 'font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.52rem;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;padding:.25rem .6rem;border:1px solid var(--amber-d);color:var(--amber-l);background:rgba(168,108,16,.08);cursor:pointer'

: 'font-family:\'IBM Plex Mono\',monospace;font-size:.52rem;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;padding:.25rem .6rem;border:1px solid var(--rule);color:var(--ink-soft);background:none;cursor:pointer'

}

// Update lens target buttons

document.querySelectorAll('.conv-lens-target').forEach(btn => {

const active = btn.dataset.id === id

btn.style.borderColor = active ? 'var(--amber-d)' : 'var(--rule)'

btn.style.color = active ? 'var(--amber-l)' : 'var(--ink-soft)'

btn.style.background = active ? 'rgba(168,108,16,.08)' : 'none'

})

}


function buildConvLensTargets() {

const container = document.getElementById('convLensTargets')

if (!container) return

// Show the 5 most recognisable lenses as quick targets

const quick = ['hemingway','villeneuve','coens','sorkin','pinter']

container.innerHTML = quick.map(id => {

const lens = LENS_DATA[id]
